content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — Camera usage string is generic and location string is a literal "TODO" placeholder (Info.plist: NSCameraUsageDescription, NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription).
Pierre: Apple rejects apps whose purpose strings don't clearly explain why a permission is needed for a specific feature — "This app needs camera access." is boilerplate, and "TODO" is an unshipped placeholder that will trigger immediate rejection. Also, no location usage appears anywhere in ScannerView.swift or the rest of the project, suggesting this permission may be unused/copy-pasted. Fix the camera string to reference document scanning specifically (e.g., "SnapScan uses the camera to scan and digitize your documents") and either remove the location key if unused or write a real, feature-specific description.
